mentions.note: Cramer favorite; only healthcare bright spot for months; healthcare sector now broadening beyond just Lilly.

Core holding 5+ years, up 360%+. Retatrutide phase 3 data (70 lb weight loss) superior to current GLP-1s; VERVE-102 gene therapy shows 62% LDL reduction with single infusion. Three vaccine acquisitions diversify beyond GLP-1. CVS Caremark deal expands access ($25–50/month). Revenue/EPS CAGR 25%+ continues; secular growth play insulated from macro turmoil.
Multiple GLP-1 formulations including novel muscle-sparing candidate; knockout punch vs. Novo Nordisk if approved. Healthcare sector contrarian pick.

Only healthcare company with trillion-dollar valuation; GLP-1 drugs (diabetes, weight loss) huge growth; new weight loss drug retatrutide showed strong phase-3 data (busts fat not muscle); separate gene therapy for cholesterol reduction shows great data; brilliant management continues to deliver.
Pharma winner today; among sectors benefiting from rotation out of overheated data center names; healthcare sector solid.
Cramer did extensive late-night work; ready to roll; bull market for Lilly; willing to sit down with company; very strong conviction.
Up 10% on fantastic quarter; new GLP-1 pill Zounandea exceeding expectations with >20k users despite slow start rumor; strong execution.

FDA approved Orforglipron GLP-1 pill with superior convenience vs. Novo; $25/month with coverage; blockbuster potential. Plus $7.8B Synaptic acquisition for orexin neurological R&D (narcolepsy, addiction, mood disorders) using AI. Own it.
In 'giving-up-edness' moment on GLP-1 pill; one of greatest drug companies in history; GLP-1 and orals will be huge; buy on weakness.
Co-innovation lab with Nvidia on digital biology and drug discovery; AI-driven life sciences could extend human longevity; beneficiary of Nvidia's inference and agent platform expansion.
Biotech leader; Cramer cited as peer exception to Regeneron (implies strong position). Paired with Regeneron as superior drug stock.
Pharmaceutical company excels at drug innovation and development; GLP-1/obesity drug portfolio strong; better thesis than Novo Nordisk's price-cutting approach.
What drives stock is GLP-1 clinical trial reports not earnings; could announce new data and get stock rolling; reports Wednesday morning.
Dominating GLP-1 market with strong pill form pipeline. David Ricks executing flawlessly. Preferred over Novo Nordisk in caller discussion.
Cramer's favorite pharma CEO Dave Rich; Eli Lilly launching oral GLP-1 in spring 2026; not going against owning Novo but bullish on Lilly competition in GLP-1 space.
GLP-1 play; higher P/E multiple; money managers passing on it in pullback scenario; deserves strength but too risky in weak economy thesis.
Opened AI-drug facility with Nvidia; obesity drug leader; still gold standard in GLP-1 market; CEO Dave Rex presented at JPMorgan Healthcare Conference.
Fastest-growing pharma; but J&J spin-off more attractive on valuation uplift potential from low-growth business separation.
Earnings coming through, GLP-1 drugs ramping, new factories built; want huge position; stay in stock despite high share price.
Strong pharmaceutical company with superior growth profile; growth rate justifies premium valuation relative to peers.
Cited as one of the great momentum stocks requiring forward-looking price targets; GLP-1 sales opportunity extends through end of decade, justifying premium multiples.
